subject: Cloud Computing
title: Serverless Computing
description: Learn Serverless Computing in one minute.

hook.text: What if you could run code without managing any servers at all? That's the serverless promise.

buildup.text: With serverless, you upload a function. The cloud runs it when triggered, then shuts it down.

discovery.text: AWS Lambda launched in 2014. You pay per execution — even a millisecond counts, nothing more.

twist.text: Serverless still uses servers. You just never see or manage them. The name is marketing genius.

climax.text: Serverless is ideal for bursty, unpredictable workloads. It's overkill for steady, constant tasks.

punchline.text: Serverless has servers. You're just not invited to see them.

quiz.question: What does 'serverless computing' actually mean?
options: The cloud provider manages all servers — you only provide the code
Computing that runs without any physical servers at all
A server that turns itself off permanently after one use
